practicalExamples.description: Use AI to categorize and assign incoming Slack messages to appropriate team members based on content and urgency, with memory of past interactions to avoid duplicate responses.
Automate customer follow-ups in CRM systems by using AI memory to track conversation history and personalize messages, ensuring no lead falls through the cracks.
Implement AI-powered support ticket routing that remembers previous issues and customer preferences to assign tickets to the most suitable agent with relevant context.

implementationSteps.action: Identify a high-volume, repetitive workflow that requires decision-making based on context (e.g., customer inquiries, order processing).
Map out the workflow steps, including all decision points and required data inputs, using tools like flowcharts or process mapping software.
Design a memory system that captures and stores relevant context for each workflow instance, using databases or specialized AI memory solutions.
Implement approval gates and quality checks at critical decision points to ensure AI actions align with business policies and standards.
Set up logging and monitoring to track AI decisions, performance metrics, and identify areas for improvement or potential issues.
Establish clear failure handling procedures, including fallback processes and human intervention triggers when AI cannot make confident decisions.
Connect workflow outcomes to measurable business metrics (e.g., response time, customer satisfaction, error rates) to validate ROI and guide optimization.
